steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The researchers' explanation depends on showing an unplanned purchase that results from customers moving through the store while searching for relocated items. The survey response that best supports this explanation must demonstrate this exact cause-and-effect chain.

steps.title: Apply to the survey responses
steps.content: Choice A demonstrates it: a customer came for cleaning supplies, couldn't find them in their usual location, searched through the store, and bought an unplanned notebook along the way. Choices B, C, and D don't work—B shows searching without unplanned purchases, C shows a coupon driving the purchase (the explicit contrast mechanism), and D involves store selection rather than layout effects. That matches choice A — A is correct.
